ac_uid_algorithm: sha256(type + given + when + then + json.dumps(check, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False))[:16]

items.then: key 'test-writer' MUST exist with allowed_tools containing the full 8-tool list [Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Skill] (Write/Edit/MultiEdit required for test-writer to actually write test files); denied_write_path_prefixes mirroring other non-core specialists; rationale non-empty

items.given: hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py and hooks/prompt-workflow.py and commands/dev.md are read as text
items.when: the CP_AGENTS set literal, the agent_types list literal, and the fallback agent-enumeration loop are inspected for test-writer membership
items.then: all three sites MUST list test-writer so the harness consistently treats it as a dispatchable agent (without the third commands/dev.md fallback site, sessions resumed without the hook auto-init still skip test-writer registry creation)

checks.description: test-writer literal MUST appear inside the CP_AGENTS set literal body
test-writer literal MUST appear inside the agent_types list literal body
test-writer MUST appear inside the fallback `for agent in ... ; do` agent-enumeration loop (near line 133-136) so the manual init path also creates the test-writer.json sentinel

items.when: the Procedure section step 5 paragraph and the Output JSON Report code-fence are inspected (scope-anchored)
items.then: step 5 instructs writing the per-task active manifest at tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json AND maintaining the global index file at tests/generated/manifest.json with shape {kind:"index", tasks:[...]}; the report.manifest_path field points at the per-task path; the residual Cycle-1-drift wording 'Write `tests/generated/manifest.json`' (with backticks) MUST NOT appear unqualified in step 5

must_not_contain_regex_scoped.description: Within procedure step 5 paragraph only, the unqualified 'Write tests/generated/manifest.json' wording (with or without backticks) MUST NOT appear

items.when: Phase 5 (section heading '#### Phase 5: Manifest Verification') and the output JSON schema's manifest_verification block are inspected (scope-anchored)
items.then: Phase 5 step 1 MUST read the per-task manifest (not the global file); pytest invocation MUST scope to tests/generated/<task_id>/; schema manifest_path MUST be per-task; the LEGITIMATE global-index presence sentinel at line ~1004 MUST be preserved verbatim

must_not_contain_regex_scoped.description: The Cycle-1-drift active_tests-from-global phrasing MUST NOT appear inside the Phase 5 section

check._doc_preserves_sentinel: The 'test_writer_expected = true AND tests/generated/manifest.json is missing' sentinel at qa.md ~line 1004 is a LEGITIMATE global-index presence check (the global index is the {kind:index, tasks[]} file the test-writer continues to write under Option B). It MUST remain; verified by the fourth must_contain_regex above.

items.when: the Step 11 QA dispatch block and the Dev-handoff text block (~line 666) are inspected (scope-anchored); the test-writer dispatch input block (~line 633) MAY continue to mention the global index file
items.then: every manifest path passed onward to Dev or QA MUST be parametrized as tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json; the global file MAY still be cited as the index but only with an explicit '(index)' or equivalent disambiguation

must_not_contain_regex_scoped.description: Inside Step 11 (QA dispatch), unqualified 'tests/generated/manifest.json' references MUST NOT remain (must be parametrized as per-task or annotated '(index)' / '(global)')
Inside the Dev-handoff text (~line 666), unqualified 'tests/generated/manifest.json' MUST be replaced with per-task form or annotated

items.given: the git working tree is queried after dev has run `git rm --cached -- agent-scores.json agent-scores.json.lock .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/` (derived-work cleanup required because M-P2.3 .gitignore is a no-op for already-tracked files)
items.then: stdout is empty AND exit_code 0 AND the working-tree copies of agent-scores.json (+ .lock) AND every .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/cp-state-*.json file still exist on disk (verifying dev used --cached, NOT a destructive `git rm`)

assertions.description: Working-tree copies MUST survive — proves dev used `git rm --cached` (not `git rm` / `git rm -r` without --cached)

check._scope_label: DERIVED_WORK: this AC is the necessary side-effect of M-P2.3 .gitignore append on already-tracked files; not a separate user-stated requirement. Out_of_scope_observation #1 in ba-qa-report-20260520-221452.json flags that the orchestrator should pre-authorize git rm --cached explicitly. BA proceeded under the spec Section-7 P2.3 mandate that the patterns 'belong in .gitignore from inception'.

items.given: README.md, INDEX.md, .claude/INDEX.md are read as text
items.then: total count across all three files MUST be 0

items.given: hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py and hooks/doc_sync/tree.py are read as text
items.when: the SKIP_NAMES / SKIP_FILES / IGNORE_DIRS literal definitions are inspected (line-anchored, structural)
items.then: the SKIP_NAMES set in regen_readme.py MUST contain both 'agent-scores.json' AND 'agent-scores.json.lock'; the SKIP_FILES set in tree.py MUST contain both 'agent-scores.json' AND 'agent-scores.json.lock'; .claude/specs MUST be excluded via IGNORE_DIRS, an additional set, or an equivalent runtime filter wired into the actual exclusion code path (not as a passive comment)

checks.description: SKIP_NAMES literal MUST be a Python set containing BOTH 'agent-scores.json' AND 'agent-scores.json.lock' (order-independent — see also the second regex variant below)
Alternate ordering accepted: .lock first then .json; satisfies the same structural intent
SKIP_FILES literal in tree.py MUST contain BOTH 'agent-scores.json' AND 'agent-scores.json.lock' (or alternate ordering — see below)

.claude/specs MUST be excluded via IGNORE_DIRS literal containing '.claude/specs', OR via an equivalent runtime exclusion (e.g., gitignore-aware filter, or a specs-skip mechanism). Bare passive comments do not satisfy.

check._grouping_doc: AC-09 accepts either ordering of agent-scores.json + .lock inside SKIP_NAMES/SKIP_FILES; treat the two ordering checks per file as an OR group (at least one must match).

items.given: scripts/score-update.sh, scripts/score-inject.sh, scripts/canary-verify.sh are read as text
items.when: every non-comment occurrence of 'python3' is inspected; for each invocation, the same logical line or its immediately enclosing subshell/function scope MUST first execute `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate` (precedence verification, not raw count)
items.then: every python3 invocation is preceded in its own shell scope by venv activation. The recommended canonical shape (and the only form this AC accepts inline) is the subshell wrap: `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 ... )`. Verification: for each non-comment line containing python3, that line OR a preceding line within the same subshell (between unmatched `(` ... `)`) MUST contain `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate`

checks.description: Every non-comment line that calls python3 MUST be part of a subshell that first sources the venv on the SAME logical line (or be inside an explicit subshell where activation precedes python3 in the same `( ... )` block)
All 3 python3 sites (~lines 95, 106, 125) MUST be wrapped

check._verification_algorithm: (1) For each file, scan line-by-line. (2) Find every line whose stripped form does NOT start with '#' and contains the literal token 'python3' as a word (not 'python3-config' / 'python3.10' command substring). (3) For each such line, assert the SAME line matches the regex `\(\s*source\s+(?:~|\$\{?HOME\}?|/root)/\.claude/venv/bin/activate\s*&&\s*python3\b`. (4) If a python3 invocation is not on the same line as the activation (e.g., multi-line heredoc form), the test MAY accept if the line is inside a `( ... )` subshell whose opening line contains `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate` AND no intervening `)` closes that subshell before the python3 line. (5) Comments and bare-line activations without a python3 on the same line do NOT count toward satisfaction.
check._rationale: OBJ-08 PROPOSED_FIX: bind dev to the subshell-wrap form so the check is a simple inline regex. Eliminates the count-vs-precedence drift and the comment-bypass risk.

items.when: the decimal Sub-step labels and the cross-references at former lines 844 and 1115 are inspected
items.then: no 'Sub-step 12.\d' label remains anywhere in the file; the cross-reference formerly at line 844 (after Sub-step 12.0 / spec section updates block) now refers to integer-step prose like 'Step 12 spec section updates' or 'After applying the spec section updates above,'; the cross-reference formerly at line 1115 (in the Mascot Score Changes summary section) now refers to integer-step prose like 'Step 12 score-update events' or 'score-update events from Step 12'

checks.description: No decimal Sub-step 12.N label remains anywhere in the file
Cross-reference replacing the former 'After Sub-step 12.0 completes' phrasing (around former line 844) MUST exist as integer-step prose referring to the spec-section-updates block of Step 12

Cross-reference replacing the former 'from Sub-step 12.1' phrasing (around former line 1115, in the Mascot Score Changes section) MUST exist as integer-step prose anchoring the score-update events to Step 12

items.then: exit code MUST be 1 (no matches); both temp files via mktemp

items.when: the Standard 6 section (between heading '### Standard 6: English Only' and the next '### Standard 7' heading) is extracted as scoped text
items.then: within the scoped Standard 6 section there MUST exist a NEW exemption paragraph distinct from the existing 'Exemption (verbatim user-binding quotes)' at line ~320; the new paragraph carries a stable anchor heading containing the literal phrase 'Cycle-2 user-facing'; AND the paragraph contains ALL of: (a) literal 'spec-20260518-225715 §5.1' attribution, (b) at least one of the five Chinese rank-label samples from spec §5.1, (c) the literal phrase 'AskUserQuestion', (d) re-affirmation that code comments AND stderr/diagnostic strings remain English-only

scoped_assertions.description: Stable anchor heading for the NEW exemption paragraph (distinct from the existing 'verbatim user-binding quotes' exemption at line ~320)

scoped_assertions.regex: 见习学徒|初级工匠|熟练工匠|资深工匠|宗师级
scoped_assertions.description: At least one of the five spec §5.1 rank-label samples
Re-affirmation that BOTH code comments AND stderr/diagnostic strings remain English-only (not a single one-sided clause)
